summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the workflow import endpoint at GET /workflows/from-url allows SSRF by making server-side requests to arbitrary URLs without validation. The PoC demonstrates accessing a simulated internal metadata endpoint that should not be accessible from external requests.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should start a simulated internal metadata server, trigger the SSRF vulnerability via the workflow import endpoint, and successfully retrieve data from the internal-only endpoint, demonstrating unauthorized access to internal services.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction successfully built the n8n environment, started a fake internal metadata server on port 9999, authenticated to n8n, and used the /workflows/from-url endpoint to fetch data from the internal URL. The server successfully retrieved the fake metadata, confirming SSRF vulnerability.
verification_method: The run script starts a fake internal metadata server on port 9999, then checks if the SSRF exploit successfully accessed this internal-only endpoint and created a proof file. This distinguishes actual SSRF from connection errors or unrelated failures.
evidence_produced_by_run: The internal metadata server received a request from the n8n backend
The workflow import endpoint successfully fetched data from http://localhost:9999/metadata
No SSRF protection (IP validation, allowlist, DNS rebinding check) prevented the request
The fake metadata containing sensitive-looking credentials was retrieved successfully
limitations_or_blockers: The PoC uses a local endpoint (localhost:9999) instead of cloud metadata endpoints for safety
In a real environment, this would work with actual internal services or cloud metadata APIs

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh copies the target repository from the session directory and mounts that copy into the container at /target-repo.
